---
title: "Lean Management in Emergency Departments"
description: "A consulting report applying Lean methodologies to improve patient flow and reduce waiting times in an Irish hospital emergency department."
date: 2024-12-01
categories: [Lean Management, Healthcare, Consulting, VSM]
image: ../images/hospital.png
---

## Overview

This project was completed as part of the **Advanced Operational Modelling** module at Dublin City University during my Erasmus exchange. The objective was to act as a management consultant and propose Lean-based improvements for a fictitious Irish hospital emergency department.

## The Problem

::: {.callout-note}
Emergency departments across Ireland face significant pressure from increasing patient volumes, staff shortages, and inefficient processes — leading to long waiting times and patient dissatisfaction.
:::

## Methodology

Three core Lean tools were applied:

- **Value Stream Mapping (VSM)** — to identify waste and bottlenecks in the patient journey
- **Kaizen** — to propose continuous improvement initiatives
- **5S** — to reorganise physical spaces and improve efficiency

## Key Findings

| Issue | Impact |
|-------|--------|
| Redundant triage steps | Increased waiting time |
| Poor bed management | Patient flow bottlenecks |
| Disorganised storage | Staff time wasted |

## Recommendations

1. Implement a fast-track pathway for low-acuity patients
2. Introduce real-time bed management dashboard
3. Apply 5S methodology to medication and equipment storage
4. Run regular Kaizen events with frontline staff

## Key Takeaways

This project strengthened my ability to apply structured problem-solving frameworks to real-world healthcare operations — directly relevant to my career goals in MOA/PMO roles in the health sector.
